meta.archetype: the cheap mark - 0-cost fuel that opens the hex and digs
meta.nearestExisting: Sense_Vitals.json (the shipped 'inflict Vulnerable + draw a card' shape); Gaze_into_the_Beyond.json (0-cost cantrip banking a status)
meta.whyDifferent: Sense_Vitals is Monster-rarity/1 Neutral and stacks Anger alongside; this is the reward-pool 0-cost on-ramp for the Vulnerable archetype - the smallest possible mark (1) plus a cantrip so the hex deck never floods on setup. inflict:1 + referenceStatus Vulnerable is Shieldbreaker/Sunder_Armor/Sense_Vitals verbatim (referenceStatus PathID 15608).
meta.intendedDeck: Every Malediction hand - the cheap opener that starts the +10%/stack amp and replaces itself. Pull-safe as a plain 0-cost cantrip for any INT deck.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost common par ~2-3 value (CORPUS-STATS #1): 1 Vulnerable (~1) + draw 1 (~1.5). No uncertainty: inflict + referenceStatus Vulnerable and draw:1 are both shipped-verbatim.

cards.name: Weave the Hex
cards.description: This combat, whenever you play a Magic card, inflict 1 Vulnerable.

meta.archetype: the ramp engine - a spell-dense turn snowballs the mark
meta.nearestExisting: Hidden_Flurry.json (EndAction + played-card condition, the verbatim structure); Packed With Nails / Chain Reaction (DC.PowderAndPatience: combat blessing, enchant:self on play, EndAction effect gated by a played-card condition)
meta.whyDifferent: The shipped per-play enchantments gate on a keyword (bomb) or a Sinister card; this gates on card TYPE (Magic) to make every spell deepen the hex - the reason a Malediction deck wants to chain cheap spells. Structure (enchant:self combat blessing + EndAction payload) is Packed-With-Nails-verbatim; the payload inflict:1 + referenceStatus Vulnerable is shipped-verbatim.
meta.intendedDeck: The core engine: with Baleful Eye + cheap appliers, every Action tick adds a stack, compounding the +10%/stack amp toward the cap.
meta.powerNotes: R 2 INT engine par (CORPUS-STATS #1: Rare 2-cost). UNVERIFIED (rank 2): the ActionType 'Is magic' gate on an EndAction enchantment effect has no sibling-pack precedent - the ActionType enum + CheckType reader are confirmed in source (ConditionChecker.cs:266-267,697) but the pairing is novel. Fallback: tag the pack's Magic cards with a shared cardKeyword and gate via PlayedCardHasKeyword (Packed-With-Nails-verbatim, fully verified).

meta.archetype: the finisher - the mark cashed as one arcane strike
meta.nearestExisting: Sunder_Armor.json (quickvalue:[[other(status)Armor]] - shipped foe-status read into a value); Doomwritten (DC.Cartomancer: statusdamage:[[other(status)Doom]]:arcane, foe-status read turned into damage)
meta.whyDifferent: First card that reads Vulnerable stacks for damage (0 shipped Vulnerable readers - scripted scan). The read is a VERIFIED-GENERIC token, not a novel risk: GameTextTranslator.CountStacks (GameTextTranslator.cs:2150-2169) strips [[other(status)X]] to the bare status name and scans the foe's lasting effects by name, returning stacks or 0 - the same path Doom/Bleeding/Armor use, generic over any status.
meta.intendedDeck: The Malediction cash-out: dump a deep mark. Note Vulnerable also amps this hit (+10%/stack incoming), so it double-scales - deliberately kept to a base of 2 for honesty.
meta.powerNotes: U 1 INT (CORPUS-STATS #1: 1-cost U between C~4 and R~5). Floor 2 (unmarked); with a mid mark of 3-5 Vulnerable, ~5-7 base before the amp. BUDGET FLAG (rank 1 - the pack's swinginess risk): base + amp double-dips (at cap 10 Vulnerable, 12 base x +100% = 24); base held to 2 so early value is honest; tighten the coefficient at gate 3 if the ceiling proves hot. damage:X+[[token]]:arcane is The-Cut/Prophetic-Bolt-verbatim form.

meta.archetype: the damage-forward chipper - tempo that also marks
meta.nearestExisting: Shieldbreaker.json (C: 3 damage + inflict Vulnerable - the exact mechanic, 1 STR/reward-excluded); the 4-dmg 1-cost common baseline
meta.whyDifferent: Shieldbreaker is the physical STR armor-break twin; Rend the Veil ports the damage+mark shape into arcane/INT and shifts the split toward tempo (3 dmg, 1 mark) so it is never a dead card while still feeding the hex. Both codeLines shipped-verbatim (damage:N:arcane = Cartomancer's-Edge form; inflict + referenceStatus Vulnerable = Shieldbreaker).
meta.intendedDeck: The reliable Magic body that also ticks Weave the Hex and nudges the amp; pull-safe as a fair 1-INT arcane hit anywhere.
meta.powerNotes: C 1 INT: 3 arcane (~at the 4-dmg median once the 1 Vulnerable rider is priced) + 1 Vulnerable. No uncertainty.

cards.name: The Withering
cards.description: This combat, the foe takes double damage from Poison, Burning and Bleeding.

enchantment.text: Takes double damage from Poison, Burning and Bleeding.

meta.archetype: BRIDGE hex -> afflictions - the marked foe rots faster
meta.nearestExisting: Flammable.json (the only shipped `vulnerable`-codeLine card: enchant, codeLine 'vulnerable' + referenceStatus Burning = double that status's ticks); Hex_of_Vulnerability.json (enchant:other onto the foe)
meta.whyDifferent: The `vulnerable` codeLine ships in exactly one card (Flammable, Monster) and doubles a named affliction's tick damage (GAME-MECHANICS Part II ~L244; SpellEffects StatusDamage). This is the first PLAYER card to wield it, and it welds the pack's amp to DoT: Vulnerable's +10%/stack incoming multiplies the already-doubled Poison/Burning/Bleeding ticks. Deliberate bridge to the sibling DoT packs (VenomousLegacy Poison/Bleed, EmberweaveGrove Burning). enchant:other = Hex_of_Vulnerability-verbatim; codeLine 'vulnerable' + referenceStatus = Flammable-verbatim.
meta.intendedDeck: Hex + DoT splash (or the full mod sandbox where Poison/Burning/Bleeding sources exist). A build-around, not a standalone card - dead in a pure-Vulnerable deck by design (user-chosen: keep the novel hook as a bridge).
meta.powerNotes: R 2 INT bridge engine. UNVERIFIED (rank 3): target direction - Flammable applies the curse to its holder (theType 2); we apply via enchant:other so the doubling must attach to the FOE's incoming DoT. Verify in gate-4 that the foe-applied `vulnerable` curse doubles the ticks THEY take. Fallback if direction misfires: self-contained doubler inflict:[[other(status)Vulnerable]] Vulnerable (Strangling-Lashes/Sealed-Fate idiom) - the mark deepens, no DoT dependency.

meta.archetype: the mark as defense - BRIDGE to Seeker survival
meta.nearestExisting: Hex_of_Vulnerability.json (bless:[[hexes]] - a status-count read into a bless); Diviner's Weapon (DC.Cartomancer: bless:1 Barrier)
meta.whyDifferent: The first defensive card gated on the foe's own mark: the deeper you have cursed them, the safer you are - the Seeker-side use of the same stack count the offense reads. bless:[[token]] + referenceStatus Barrier is the Hex-of-Vulnerability read shape + the shipped Barrier idiom; the [[other(status)Vulnerable]] read is verified-generic (CountStacks, GameTextTranslator.cs:2150-2169).
meta.intendedDeck: The survival lane while the hex ramps; turns a stalled control turn into mitigation. Pull-safe scaling defense for any INT deck that inflicts Vulnerable.
meta.powerNotes: U 1 INT: Barrier = current foe Vulnerable (0 if unmarked - honest whiff mode), realistically 2-5 mid-combat. Comparable to a 1-cost conditional Armor/Barrier (Brace/Block family). Read verified-generic; no fresh risk beyond the shared Vulnerable-read note.

cards.name: Deepening Dread
cards.description: Inflict 2 Vulnerable. If the foe already has 5 or more, inflict 2 more.

meta.archetype: the accelerant - the mark deepens faster once it's deep
meta.nearestExisting: Shieldbreaker.json (flat Vulnerable applier); Rupture (DC.VenomousLegacy: AnyValue IsMoreThan [[other(status)Bleeding]]:4 threshold rider, the verbatim gate)
meta.whyDifferent: First Vulnerable applier with a self-referential threshold: a base 2 that jumps to 4 once the foe is at 5+, rewarding the deck that has committed to the curse and pushing toward the +100% cap. Threshold idiom (AnyValue IsMoreThan [[other(status)X]]:N) is Rupture-verbatim; inflict + referenceStatus Vulnerable is shipped-verbatim; the read is verified-generic (CountStacks).
meta.intendedDeck: Mid-curve accelerant in any Malediction deck; the card that tips a maturing mark toward the cap.
meta.powerNotes: U 1 INT: 2 Vulnerable floor, 4 past the gate. The rarity step buys the conditional (METHODOLOGY 4.1.3, the whiff mode IS the sub-threshold case). No fresh risk (threshold read verified-generic).

cards.name: Sign of Ruin
cards.description: This combat, while the foe has 5 or more Vulnerable, your Actions deal 2 additional damage.

meta.archetype: the payoff plateau - a deep mark turns every strike up
meta.nearestExisting: Rigged_Explosives.json (adddamage enchantment gated by a card condition); The Significator (DC.Cartomancer: CalculateOutgoingDamage adddamage engine)
meta.whyDifferent: Shipped adddamage enchantments gate on your own state (a keyword, a stack count); Sign of Ruin gates the whole combat's outgoing bonus on the FOE's mark depth - the deck that reaches 5+ Vulnerable flips a permanent +2. Payout idiom (CalculateOutgoingDamage adddamage) is Significator/Cruel-Intent-verbatim; the foe-status gate is the Rupture threshold read (verified-generic, CountStacks).
meta.intendedDeck: The mid-game plateau: once the hex is deep, every Action (weapon swings included) gains +2 on top of the +10%/stack amp.
meta.powerNotes: R 2 INT engine (CORPUS-STATS #1: Rare 2-cost). UNVERIFIED (rank 2): no shipped enchant gates a CalculateOutgoingDamage effect on a foe-status threshold - both halves individually verbatim, the pairing novel. Fallback: the ungated Cruel-Intent form (flat +1), or move the read onto a scaling adddamage:[[other(status)Vulnerable]]/5.

meta.archetype: capstone - the whole curse collected at once
meta.nearestExisting: Strangling_Lashes.json (inflict:[[other(status)Doom]] - shipped status-read payload); Sealed Fate (DC.Cartomancer: L 3 INT status-read capstone); Metastasis (DC.VenomousLegacy: L consume-the-foe's-statuses)
meta.whyDifferent: The Vulnerable finisher the pack's ramp exists to feed: damage equal to twice the mark, one shot. Distinct from Sealed Fate (Doom, no self-exile) and Metastasis (reads [[otherAfflictions]] - which Vulnerable is NOT a member of, theType other, so this must read [[other(status)Vulnerable]] explicitly). Bounded: OneUse + exile + single copy, no loop. damage:M*[[token]]:arcane form is shipped multiplicative token math (NumberParser); read verified-generic (CountStacks).
meta.intendedDeck: The kill button after a full hex ramp; also double-amplified by Vulnerable's incoming boost, so it is deliberately gated behind L rarity, 3 INT, OneUse and exile.
meta.powerNotes: L 3 INT (CORPUS-STATS #1: L 3-cost 8-15). Floor 0 if the foe is unmarked (a dead draw - the honest whiff), ~10-20 at a mid-to-deep mark before the amp, capped by Vulnerable's own 10-stack ceiling (2x10=20 base). BUDGET FLAG (rank 1, shared with Comeuppance): base x amp double-dip; OneUse/exile/single-copy bound it to one detonation. Retune the multiplier at gate 3 if the ceiling proves hot.

weapons.name: Baleful Eye
weapons.description: Deal [damage:2] damage. If the foe is Vulnerable, inflict 1 Vulnerable.

meta.archetype: the snowball metronome - the mark deepens itself every swing
meta.nearestExisting: Shieldbreaker.json (gated Vulnerable applier); Forcewand.json (Arcanist basic, damage:2 statline); Cartomancer's Edge (DC.Cartomancer, retired: same Magic BasicAttack 1-Neutral statline)
meta.whyDifferent: 78-weapon sweep: no basic carries a self-referential status rider. Baleful Eye deals the par 2 arcane and, ONCE the mark exists, adds a stack every swing - so the weapon compounds the +10%/stack amp for free, but only after a card seeds it (honest floor: a plain 2-damage basic against an unmarked foe). Snowball, not metronome-modal - the opposite of the retired foretell rider. Statline = the 6-weapon curve (CORPUS-STATS #2); damage:2:arcane verbatim; the gated inflict + referenceStatus Vulnerable is Shieldbreaker's applier under a Rupture-style foe-status condition.
meta.intendedDeck: The loadout clock: Ill Omen / Whispered Curse seed the mark, then every Baleful Eye swing deepens it toward the cap. Pull-safe as a weak-but-honest 2-damage basic without any mark.
meta.powerNotes: damage 2 at 1 Neutral, Common = the starting-weapon curve exactly; identity = exactly one conditional rider, budgeted as a permanent engine (weapon addendum). The gate keeps it from stacking on an unmarked foe (no free ramp turn 1). The [[other(status)Vulnerable]] read is verified-generic (CountStacks, GameTextTranslator.cs:2150-2169), so the condition resolves; the swinginess of the amp it feeds is the gate-3 budget watch, not a mechanic risk.

weaponPowers.name: Cursebrand
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to inflict Vulnerable equal to twice your level ([[myLevel]]*2) on the foe at the start of next combat. Passive: Your Actions deal 1 additional damage to Vulnerable foes.

meta.archetype: the foe starts the fight already marked
meta.nearestExisting: Baneful Weapon (cd2: startstatus Poison 2xlvl on the foe + passive); Diviner's Weapon (DC.Cartomancer, retired: startstatus:[[myLevel]]*2:false + referenceStatus Doom, cd2, the exact template)
meta.whyDifferent: 0 of 66 shipped tier-0 talents touch Vulnerable (scripted scan). The activation opens the fight with the mark already laid (the hex was cast before the door opened - thesis in one line); the passive is the run-long in-combat identity, rewarding fighting a foe you have marked. ActivateWeapon startstatus:[[myLevel]]*2:false is Baneful/Diviner-verbatim; the passive (CalculateOutgoingDamage adddamage:1 + foe-status condition) composes two shipped-verbatim pieces.
meta.intendedDeck: The loadout opener: turn-1 the foe is at 2xlevel Vulnerable, so Comeuppance/The Hex Comes Due have a bank and the amp is live immediately. Passive also lifts vanilla INT damage vs any Vulnerable foe.
meta.powerNotes: cd2 curve point (CORPUS-STATS #3: level-scaled next-combat status opener, Baneful = Poison 2xlvl at cd2). Vulnerable 2xlvl is weaker per stack than Poison 2xlvl (a % amp vs a repeating tick) - fits cd2. Passive +1 is shipped small-rider scale (passives do not scale with cooldown). UNVERIFIED (rank 2): startstatus + Vulnerable referenceStatus has no shipped pairing (command + status both shipped; Diviner's Weapon carried the identical class of risk with Doom). Fallback: Warding-Weapon startstatus:[[myMaxHealth]]/5 self-Barrier opener, keep the passive as the identity carrier.

meta.archetype: the seed - loadout mark, turn-1 reliable
meta.nearestExisting: Shieldbreaker.json (C: 3 damage + inflict 3 Vulnerable - the exact mechanic, 1 STR/reward-excluded); Mindstrike.json (Seeker default starting card)
meta.whyDifferent: Shieldbreaker is the physical STR twin; Ill Omen is the INT/arcane loadout seed - the reliable turn-1 mark that starts the amp and completes the kit (Cursebrand pre-marks, Baleful Eye deepens, Ill Omen guarantees a stack even without the power up). Reliability-first per the starting-card addendum: both halves unconditional. damage:2:arcane + inflict:2 Vulnerable are both shipped-verbatim.
meta.intendedDeck: Loadout: Baleful Eye + Cursebrand + this. Mid-run pull: a clean 2-damage-plus-mark tempo card for any INT deck.
meta.powerNotes: Starting-card curve (CORPUS-STATS #4): cost 1 (81% mode), Common, Magic/Action, 2 effect lines, normal reward-pool card. 2 arcane + 2 Vulnerable for 1 INT - reliability over power, on curve. Near-neighbor Shieldbreaker differs by color/type/numbers (3/3 STR reward-excluded vs 2/2 INT pool). No uncertainty.
